authoritative_sources.title: Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinic Certification Criteria
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's 2023 CCBHC criteria are the federal operating baseline for public and community behavioral-health programs, defining required services, care coordination, access expectations, and documentation-sensitive workflow boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: community behavioral-health program grounding and prerequisite validation

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Benefit Policy Manual, Chapter 6: Hospital Services Covered Under Part B
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS Chapter 6 is the primary operating manual for outpatient psychiatric services, partial hospitalization, and intensive outpatient workflows, including active-treatment, physician certification, recertification, and continued-stay expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authorization packet assembly and continued-stay progression

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR § 438.210 - Coverage and authorization of services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This Medicaid managed-care rule directly governs authorization criteria, timeframes, and service-authorization handling for public behavioral-health coverage, making it a primary regulatory source for utilization workflow decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service-authorization decision timing and evidence sufficiency
used_for: blocked-state handling when required criteria are absent

authoritative_sources.title: CCBHC Quality Measures Guidance and Webinar Series
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's quality-measure guidance is a recognized federal workflow reference for behavioral-health follow-up, treatment engagement, and care-transition evidence that utilization staff must not ignore when closing public/community cases.

authoritative_sources.title: Utilization Management Accreditation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCQA is a recognized vendor-neutral standards body, and its UM accreditation program directly addresses fair and timely determinations, objective evidence-based criteria, qualified-reviewer expectations, and appeals/process controls relevant to this lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: utilization workflow control design and escalation posture

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR § 438.404 - Notice of adverse benefit determination
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This Medicaid rule is the controlling public standard for what a denial or reduction notice must contain, including reasons and appeal-rights information, which directly constrains behavioral utilization completion and rework handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: adverse-determination evidence review and notice quality control
used_for: rework triggers for deficient denial packets

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR § 482.61 - Condition of participation: Special medical record requirements for psychiatric hospitals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This psychiatric-hospital record rule is a primary federal control standard for treatment-plan, progress-note, and multidisciplinary-record integrity in behavioral-health settings, making it directly relevant to utilization evidence handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record validation and audit-ready evidence packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Fact Sheet 42 CFR Part 2 Final Rule
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS and SAMHSA's Part 2 fact sheet is the current authoritative public explanation of SUD confidentiality controls, consent, and disclosure restrictions that materially constrain behavioral utilization workflows involving protected records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: privacy-gated evidence sharing and external-review routing

workflow_stages.name: Behavioral utilization intake grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the actual behavioral-health program context, member eligibility, payer or county workflow, and whether the work item belongs to utilization rather than intake, crisis, appeals, or case-management ownership.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and evidence assembly
workflow_stages.description: Validate the minimum level-of-care, active-treatment, authorization, and privacy artifacts before the work can move into review or continued-stay execution.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Authorization and continued-stay workflow progression
workflow_stages.description: Advance, pend, return, or escalate the work based on documented reviewer state, due dates, requested information, and authoritative system-of-record status rather than on narrative guesses.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, notice, and privacy handling
workflow_stages.description: Handle adverse determination notices, record conflicts, Part 2 constraints, and missing or stale evidence without silently skipping rights-sensitive or safety-sensitive steps.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion and continuity-aware closure
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when the actual reviewer action, next-step routing, and required continuity or follow-up evidence are captured and auditable.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: member or beneficiary eligibility snapshot and referral record
authorization request, authorization number, and payer or county portal status history
level-of-care evidence such as LOCUS, CALOCUS, ASAM, or other tenant-approved behavioral assessment outputs
active-treatment plan, progress notes, continuing-stay packet, and clinician-supplied updates
partial hospitalization, intensive outpatient, residential, crisis, or community-program service context artifacts
adverse benefit determination notices, peer-review notes, appeal-rights language, and rework tickets
consent, privacy restriction, and Part 2 disclosure artifacts
step-down, discharge, aftercare appointment, and follow-up linkage evidence
exception summaries, escalation notes, and completion records
systems_of_record: behavioral-health EHR or clinical documentation platform
privacy, consent, and release-of-information tracking system

allowed: Classify behavioral utilization work as in scope, out of scope, or blocked using documented program, authorization, and evidence context.
Request or validate the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, return, rework, or complete the case based on execution state and evidence already established in authoritative systems.
Prepare evidence-backed summaries, exception notes, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating medical, legal, or payer authority.
escalate: Required tenant facts, records, or authoritative source context are missing and would change the outcome.
The packet raises crisis-risk, detox-risk, or other safety-sensitive facts that require licensed clinical handling.
Payer portal, EHR, and tenant workflow records materially conflict.
Public guidance, state Medicaid or county rules, and tenant policy materially differ on criteria, timeframes, or disclosure handling.
The work requires privacy-law interpretation, appeals adjudication, or a licensed reviewer decision.
refuse: Issue an independent medical-necessity denial, approval override, or appeal outcome without the required licensed or delegated authority.
Share SUD or other protected behavioral-health records when consent or permitted disclosure pathway is unresolved.
Mark work complete without the controlling reviewer action, authorization status, notice package, or continuity evidence.
Treat behavioral-health utilization as generic prior authorization by ignoring level-of-care, active-treatment, crisis, or community follow-up context.

domain_failure_modes: a case is advanced or closed even though the level-of-care evidence or active-treatment record is missing or stale
behavioral-specific artifacts such as ASAM, LOCUS, continued-stay, crisis follow-up, or CCBHC context are dropped in favor of generic prior-auth language
staff infer final medical necessity or denial authority from workflow ownership instead of routing to licensed or delegated reviewers
payer portal and EHR status conflicts are guessed away rather than reconciled with the authoritative record owner
adverse determination notices are treated as complete even though required reason or appeal-rights elements are missing
SUD records are shared into utilization workflows without validating Part 2 posture and consent scope
step-down or discharge utilization work is closed without continuity evidence such as aftercare or follow-up linkage

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State Medicaid and county behavioral-health authorization variation
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: The pack uses federal public baseline rules only for the outer boundary. Runtime must retrieve state, county, and payer contract specifics before final timing or criterion assertions are made.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Applicability of Part 2 to a given behavioral-health record set
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: The pack requires tenant retrieval of whether the record is maintained by or received from a Part 2 program and escalates if disclosure authority is not explicit.

notes: The pack deliberately treats licensed-review, appeal-adjudication, and privacy-interpretation authority as adjacent rather than as implied by queue ownership.

Behavioral-health specificity is enforced through level-of-care artifacts, active-treatment requirements, adverse-notice controls, community follow-up expectations, and SUD confidentiality boundaries.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack use because tenant-specific differences are surfaced as mandatory retrieval and escalation dependencies.
